sections.paragraph:
ἀγανακτέω (Aristoph. Vesp. 287+; Thu. 8, 43, 4; ins, pap; LXX, ApcrEzk [Epiph. 70, 70]) fut. 3 sg. ἀγανακτήσει Wsd 5:22; 1 aor. ἠγανάκτησα (s. ἀγανάκτησις) be indignant against what is assumed to be wrong, be aroused, indignant, angry (Bel 28 Theod.; Jos., Ant. 2, 284) Mt 21:15; 26:8; Mk 10:14; 2 Cl 19:2; Ox 1224 Fgm. 2, II, 3. W. the pers. mentioned ἀ. περί τινος at someone Mt 20:24; Mk 10:41 (cp. Pla., Ep. 7, 349d; Jos., Ant. 14, 182; B-D-F §229, 2). ἀ. ἐπί τινι (Lysias 1, 1; Isocr. 16, 49; PLond I, 44, 20 p. 34 [II b.c.]=UPZ 8, 20 ἀγανακτοῦντα ἐφʼ οἷς διετελοῦντο ἐν τοιούτῳ ἱερῷ; Wsd 12:27) at someone GPt 4:14; at someth. (Diod S 4, 63, 3 ἐπὶ τῷ γεγονότι; Appian, Macedon. 1, 3) 1 Cl 56:2; Mk 14:4 ἦσαν δέ τινες ἀγανακτοῦντες πρὸς ἑαυτούς is difficult; perh. some expressed their displeasure to each other (but elsewh. πρός introduces the one against whom the displeasure is directed: Dio Chrys. 13 [7], 43 ὁ ἄρχων ἠγανάκτησε πρός με; Socrat., Ep. 6, 7.—D reads οἱ δὲ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ διεπονοῦντο καὶ ἔλεγον). A reason for displeasure is added w. ὅτι (Herodian 3, 2, 3) Lk 13:14.—DELG. M-M. Spicq.

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

ApcrEzk ApcrEzk = Apocryphon Ezechiel—List 2

Epiph Epiph , IV–V a.d.—List 5

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular

aor. aor. = aorist

Theod. Theod. = Theodotion, revision of Greek OT, II a.d.—List 2, beg.

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

Ox Ox = POxy, but refers to fragments of documents esp. relating to the lexical data base—List 1

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

pers. pers. = person(s)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Lysias Lysias , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Isocr Isocr , IV b.c.—List 5

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

GPt GPt = Gospel of Peter—List 1

someth. someth. = something

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

perh. perh. = perhaps

Dio Chrys Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Herodian Herodian = Herodianus Historicus, III a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 5
